questions.prompt: At a point on the curve f(x) = x², the tangent line has a slope of 6. What is the x-coordinate of this point?
questions.explanation: The derivative f'(x) = 2x represents the slope of the tangent line at any point on the curve. We need to find where f'(x) = 6. Setting 2x = 6 gives x = 3. This demonstrates that the derivative geometrically represents the slope of the tangent line: at the point (3, 9), the tangent line to the parabola has a slope of 6.
